title: Phenomenon 16
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: Phenomenon 16: "Borderline Fascination // The Mania Effect"}}__NOTOC__ {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Work+Sans:ital,wght@0,400;0,500;0,600;1,400;1,500&display=swap'); /* Font */ html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: work sans; }} {{Modern Theme}} {{Transparent Theme 2}}
'''Notice''' The following document contains details that are highly speculative and/or require further examination in order to be deemed authentic. This is because subjective inserts are rampant in quantity compared to verified facts. Under the order of '''M.E.G.''', all agents without proper credentials or consent from their superiors shall refrain from editing '''Phenomenon 16''' to avoid creating a campaign of misinformation, which, in return, brings about widespread panic and potentially serious consequences for the offender.
Llyrvek Scale: '''3/5'''
'''Frequency: 2/5 (Uncommon)'''
'''Borderline Fascination''' predominantly occurs inside safe havens, though this may be one-sided, considering it may also manifest in other levels outside of that category alone (albeit unconfirmed).
'''Hazard: 4/5 (Major)'''
'''Borderline Fascination''' can cause significant harm to one's mental fortitude and, in extreme cases, irreversible damage to the brain, particularly the prefrontal cortex (as reported by some neurosurgeons).
'''Enigma: 3/5 (Unresolved)'''
The gathering of precise data still encounters numerous setbacks. This version could be considered the most informative piece of guidance for now until further identification of '''Borderline Fascination''' is conducted.
==👁 Overview== '''Phenomenon 16''', colloquially referred to as '''“Borderline Fascination”''' or '''“The Mania Effect,"''' manifests itself as strong, irresistible desires to stay inside a certain level for an indefinite amount of time. Once it has fully taken effect, individuals will experience strong feelings of serenity and exhibit symptoms similar to people affected by narcotic drugs. This can eventually spiral into impulsiveness and, therefore, lead to erratic and uncontrollable behavioral patterns. There is no warning as to when this phenomenon happens, with each case reported having occurred at different intervals from one another. Although research information on this so far has been particularly scarce, many key details seem to point towards a specific theory: Since the Backrooms is a place of familiarity, yet not at the same time, it would make sense why some lost travelers would become greatly amused by the things they saw or had never seen before. Consequently, some will begin to develop a sense of belonging to a place they find comforting, which results in serious repercussions should they happen not to know about the spiritual manipulation of this twisted reality.  There hasn’t been much [[M.E.G.]] operatives can do to deter tragic incidents like these from happening. As a result, many propaganda posters are displayed on the walls of multiple levels in an attempt to raise awareness and ensure no one stays put for too long. Temporary mental facilities are also expected to be set up to contain and treat people who are directly affected by the phenomena. If, for some reason, someone is considered “too far gone” or exhibits signs of undeterred hostility, the best way to go about that would be to terminate them completely so as to prevent further harm from spreading to innocents. ==👁 History== Prior to the phenomenon’s discovery, there had been hints about its existence during the 2000s. What was formerly an urban legend quickly became an intriguing story that shocked entire communities across the era. To this day, many can recall the mystifying memories of that moment with vivid clarity. Although it didn’t come without the cost of sacrificing a small amount of personnel, the results surely outweighed the price they had to pay. ​ Many theories seem to suggest that the expedition of [[Level 699]] paved the way for justifying '''Phenomenon 16'''’s existence. '''The 699 Effect''' was originally the designation of '''Borderline Fascination''' before it was reclassified and renamed what it is today. The catch here is that the ones that went never came back. They had become assimilated to the area and repeatedly refused to leave. No one bothered reminding them of that again, with fear of being attacked in such events. Various other cases have proliferated ever since. This has raised concerns about dangerous levels, seemingly appearing to be safe while possessing the phenomenon. Even though many researchers have come and done their jobs brilliantly, the problem of '''Phenomenon 16''' still remains at large and will continue to be so for the following years to come. ==👁 Additional Information==
Medical Reports
  • Case 001
  • Case 002
  • Case 003
{{Note|body=
Destination Referenced: [[Level 57]]
Patient Name: David J. ████████
Current Status: Partly Recovered
One of the few milder cases of '''Phenomenon 16''', the subject remained relatively stable after medical intervention. One thing to keep in mind is that David kept murmuring about a woman who would be referred to as K. He constantly mentioned how much he missed her and the guilt that has been haunting him since. When asked to be a bit more elaborative, David told the officer that K had her life taken away as a result of an accident, at the hands of some reckless driver who he said “deserved to burn in hell for eternity.” During his time in '''Level 57''', David reported seeing the shadow of K, seemingly wandering around the hallway section. This led to him following her wherever she went, in a desperate attempt to meet her once again, further enhancing the hypnotic effect of '''Phenomenon 16'''. Six operatives were dispatched to get him out. Only four survived and returned. David initially resisted but was knocked out by a member of the team. }}
{{Note|body=
Destination Referenced: [[Level 699]]
Patient Name: Emma ███████
Current Status: Critical
“Never seen anything this bad before.” - Agent [REDACTED] Emma completely rejected offers to be brought out of '''Level 699''', even threatening to kill whoever “dares to drag her back to misery.” Her friends were there to assist, yet to no avail. Based on intel of those who are still residing within '''Level 699''', Emma is lying down by the Violet Pools, completely ignoring the people who pass through her from time to time. At this rate, it is best that she be left alone to avoid unnecessary casualties. A person commented, “Don’t underestimate Emma’s strength; she can easily punch you to death if desired. Not like weapons can intimidate her either because there are always ways to counter those. She’s seen enough of the bullsh** here already and just wanted to rest, rather than keep chasing the lie that we will eventually escape somehow.” }}
{{Note|body=
Patient Name: Warren G. ███████
Current Status: Missing
Possible Location: [[Level 18]]
Test results suggested few signs of abnormality in the 14-year-old child. He's a bit hyperactive, which makes doctors suspect he has ADHD. When being questioned about how he was feeling in [[Level 4]], he simply responded that nothing there was of much meaning to him. The experience in general was monotonous and sometimes suffocating. The boy always upholds the idea of an "ideal home" where he actually belongs and feels connected to: a place straight out of fairy tales where fantasies would rule while the haunting truth is extinguished. While the staff was conducting a routine health check-up, Warren was nowhere to be found. The process of locating him is still underway, and anyone who recognizes his face is encouraged to report back to nearby M.E.G. bases. }}
==👁 Addendum==
'''''A Dearly Fragmentation''''' Admittedly, I have never excelled or accomplished any major goals at all during the last few years. My thoughts are all over the place. Every day, through every hour, it's the same thing. Again and again. Why did it have to end up like this? One small miscalculation and now all that's left of me is an obnoxious mess. If I look at myself in the mirror, the reflection will probably slap and humiliate me for who I am now, won't it? Getting advice from people, not acting on it, then complaining about "things not working out." When will that wretched cycle end? Years go by, while the misery slowly evolves into a much more sinister form. When you're happy, people call you "insensitive." When you're sad, they label you as "dramatic." Basically, if I'm true to one side, then I'm false to the other. Do they ever realize how harmful those stereotypes are? I don't give a flying fuck; whatever reasoning you use, it will always be wrong to me. Has humanity reached a new low? No wonder why the only thing that is actively sabotaging it is itself; they just refuse to acknowledge that reality. I am utterly tired. I want peace. I want people to please have at least some goddamn human decency for once. Is it that hard, huh? Perhaps the only way I'm surviving in this filthy world is to splinter my mind into pieces, then put the pieces together so that I can truly know who I am and what purpose I serve. Can't trust anyone, not even those closest to you. For all you know, they're going to turn on you as soon as your struggles are revealed. "No weakness allowed," they say. Perhaps time will decide my fate. I am beyond speechless at this point. If someone is reading this, I hope you won't go down the road that many former acquaintances of mine and society did.
Hey there. I sense your resentment. I know you're in great pain and suffering. Well... Why don't you come here with us? Don't worry. It's not a trap. You will feel the energy you once had before. You will feel the happiness you had before. And most importantly, this place shall be your new home. I'm sure you won't regret it. Deal? [[Level 434|Good]]. Embrace the '''joyfulness''' that is about to unfold... There is no turning back now.
